items.prompt: Execute the CSS dead-code removal deferred since 0.44.8 using docs/CSS_DEADCODE-2026-06-08.md (61 zero-reference candidates in styles-v8/v9). STILL PENDING: the 12 orphaned styles-v*.css file deletions (disk/git op, pauses) and the optional re-run of the dead-code scan against the consolidated 281KB styles.css. attempts NOT incremented: slice succeeded. | 2026-06-16T01:06Z queue-drain-4h slice 1: dynamic-construction vetting DONE (the gating analysis the item requires before any deletion). Left pending: remaining work is the mechanical rule deletion in styles-v8/v9.css + a runtime visual smoke (not unit-testable) + PATCH release - not auto-safe, focused pass. attempts NOT incremented: slice succeeded. See result_path. | 2026-06-17T17:07Z queue-drain-4h slice 2: slice-1 premise STALE. The 0.51.3 consolidation (faf1223, 2026-06-11) flattened the split stylesheets into a single styles.css. Verified: index.html:1286 loads only styles.css?v=0.51.3, sw.js:35 precaches only /styles.css, and no build script (package.json/sync.ps1/tools/preship.sh/scripts) references or concatenates styles-v*.css - so styles-v2..v13.css (12 files, ~290KB) are now unreferenced orphans, not build inputs. Deleting rules from styles-v8/v9 prunes files the app no longer ships, so the 06-08 candidate list (docs/CSS_DEADCODE-2026-06-08.md) no longer maps to live files. RE-SCOPED: (a) remove the 12 orphaned styles-v*.css as a batch + reword the 4 stale src/mobile-hooks*.ts 'Pairs with styles-vN.css' comments to point at styles.css; (b) if per-rule pruning is still wanted, re-run the dead-code scan against the consolidated styles.css (281KB). See yac-css-deadcode-removal-2026-06-17.md. | 2026-06-22 attended ship: slice-3 comment repoints SHIPPED in v0.52.0 (commit 1260bbf) - the 4 src/mobile-hooks*.ts Pairs-with-styles comments now point at styles.css. STILL PENDING (deferred, needs runtime visual smoke): the 12 orphaned styles-v*.css file deletions + optional dead-code re-scan of consolidated styles.css. Status kept.

items.prompt: We want this live on our site full time with a safe password block and any obfuscation we can implement to keep it hidden. I.e. not shown or connectable on our home page, but still redirected to on a page we build and decide the url for

v0.98.5 2D-retirement drift still sits in three GDD regions the D1/D2 Architecture sweep (shipped v0.98.7+v0.98.9) did not reach. Same fix shape: code-aware rewrite with fresh path:LINE (symbol) anchors against world_3d.gd / enemy_field_3d.gd, NOT a text swap. (1) HIGHEST VALUE - Design-tab Locked Decision implementation pointers read as current state and now mislead: worker auto-assignment (GDD ~line 2894) says 'Existing implementation in source/buildings/building.gd already exposes a workers field' (building.gd retired v0.98.5); forest coverage (~line 3000) cites 'gameplay.gd FOREST_COVERAGE_TARGET = 0.30'; deterministic map (~line 3032) cites 'all map-gen RNGs in gameplay.gd'. Re-point each onto the live world_3d.gd inline logic, keep the locked design intent verbatim. P2. (2) M3 roadmap deliverables (~lines 2083-2087) cite retired 2D paths: source/data/mission.gd + gameplay.gd (2083), save_picker (2084), wave_director.horde_day_announced (2086), and source/ui/briefing/ + source/ui/summary/ 'reachable from win/loss screens View Summary CTA' (2087). briefing/summary dirs are gone and the View Summary reachability died with the 2D win/loss screens. Annotate the retired items with a 'retired with the 2D layer in v0.98.5; not yet ported to 3D' note; preserve the version stamps as the historical record. Do NOT just delete the checkmarks - the work genuinely shipped. P3. (3) Architecture residuals: frame-tick example (~lines 4648, 4726) still names connect_wave_director() / gameplay win-check / wave_director horde schedule; the tile-constants-drift note S-39 (~line 4816) lists enemy_base.gd, fog_of_war.gd, noise_heatmap.gd among the five TILE_W/TILE_H redefiners - all retired v0.98.5. Refresh to live module names. P3. GDD update rule applies on the ship: bump the pill, add a descending-order changelog entry, atomic-write docs/GDD.html and tail-check </html> before publish. Solo-dev voice: no em dashes, no AI vocabulary, no 'per Nick'.

items.prompt: BR best-practices P1: replace the per-tile immediate-mode ground render in iso_grid.gd (_draw_ground / _draw_ground_chunks / _draw_centered_texture) with a Godot TileMapLayer + TileSet, BR256Grass authored as a 2x2-cell tile. Editor-correct render path AND the fix for the per-frame ground-draw lag. Author the TileSet/TileMapLayer in the editor; wire iso_grid to populate from the terrain model and retire the _draw ground path. Keep the terrain model intact. See docs/GODOT_BEST_PRACTICES_ADHERENCE.md s3/s4/s8.
items.notes: | 2026-06-19T17:15Z queue-drain-4h: read-only grounding slice. The grass-ground TileMapLayer migration this P1 names is largely DONE in v0.58.x (BR HEAD 3740eb0 v0.58.42): authored TileMapLayer child _ground_tilemap (iso_grid.gd:192) filled from the terrain model by rebuild_ground_tilemap() (1076) using BR256Grass as GROUND_SOURCE_ID; _draw_ground early-returns for the default chunked-grass case (1062-1063), so the per-frame grass draw lag is resolved. Prompt is STALE: _draw_ground_chunks no longer exists (replaced by rebuild_ground_tilemap). _draw() still loops per visible tile for OVERLAYS only (deposits, edge tint, flat-mode diamonds, grid, and the ROCK-base Skirmish preview via _draw_centered_texture) - not the grass fill, not the P1 lag target. RECOMMENDATION at result_path: re-scope to the narrower overlay-migration and downgrade P1->P3 once Nick confirms the lag is gone via profiler; left P1/pending pending that call. attempts NOT incremented: investigation succeeded, no failure. Cannot author further: the TileSet/TileMapLayer is already authored and editor work is unavailable in an unattended drain.

items.prompt: BR best-practices P1: convert per-frame group polling to event/tick. ~54 global.get_cached_nodes_in_group() + 8 raw get_nodes_in_group() drive _process loops walking units/enemies/buildings groups every frame. Replace with signal/event-driven updates or a throttled tick. Prime lag suspect. s8.
items.notes: | 2026-06-20T01:10Z queue-drain-4h: read-only grounding slice. Verified 60 call sites of get_cached_nodes_in_group/get_nodes_in_group across 22 .gd files, but the 'drive _process loops every frame' premise is substantially STALE. Only 2 of 60 sites are directly in _process: auto_pause.gd:117 (already timer-throttled via _enemy_vision_timer/ENEMY_VISION_CHECK_PERIOD, early-break) and fog_of_war.gd:275 (per-frame walk of the SMALL 'units' group, guarded by a moved-only continue since v0.35.4; 4Hz cell recompute decoupled v0.74.37). The rest live in already-throttled ticks (_tick_target_acquisition(bucket_dt) enemy_pool.gd:609, _apply_hum_tick(step_dt) command_post.gd:297) or camera-range-gated updates (iso_grid.gd:571/547) or one-shot/event paths. No genuine per-frame group walk left to convert; no safe edit. RECOMMEND down-scoping this P1 to the single fog_of_war 'units' residual or closing as superseded by shipped perf work (decision-gated, P1 not auto-closed). Grounding at .work-queue-results/br-bp-polling-to-event-2026-06-16.md. attempts NOT incremented: slice succeeded.

items.prompt: Add two preship guards to scripts/release.ps1: (1) refuse a pill bump whose new version lacks a matching GDD changelog-footer entry; (2) refuse a CHANGELOG top entry whose body is a bare 'maintenance pass' / 'update ... (N files)' auto-message. Both would have caught this week's blow-out at the gate. Model on the existing Test-GddIntegrity / version-marker guards in the gauntlet. Needs a focused session plus a headless-test pass. Solo-dev voice in any message strings.
items.notes: Done 2026-06-23: scripts/check_release_descriptive.py implements both guards (footer-matches-pill + descriptive CHANGELOG top); wired into preship.ps1 and the br-drift-check Stop hook.

items.body: MED data-integrity. scripts/write-dashboard-status.ps1 only runs inside push-to-github.ps1, so an uncommitted YaAg tree leaves status/data/Agents.json stale and workTreeClean wrong (it reported v0.6.0 / clean:true while the tree was dirty at v0.7.1). Make the writer stamp workTreeClean from a live `git status --porcelain` at write time (it already computes $dirty at line 25 but only persists on push), or warn when the tree is dirty. Optionally add a standalone refresh path so the dashboard updates without a full push. File: X:\YesAndAgents\scripts\write-dashboard-status.ps1.
items.notes: Auto-surfaced by the first YaAg bar-raise. Resolved-once-pushed for the version, but the writer logic gap persists.

items.body: MED dependency. Remote access depends on cloudflared + a Cloudflare Access app that nothing in-repo pins for recovery (start-all.ps1:21 runs `cloudflared tunnel run agents`; ARCHITECTURE.md:20 references a hardcoded tunnel id + C:\Users\Kane\.cloudflared\config.yml + the Access policy). Add docs/TUNNEL-RECOVERY.md (or an ARCHITECTURE.md section) with the tunnel id, config.yml contents, and Access policy so remote access is rebuildable after machine/config loss. CAUTION: TheProphetKane/yesandagents is a PUBLIC repo. Do NOT commit the cloudflared credentials JSON/token (secret). Tunnel id + config structure + Access policy shape are non-secret; Nick to confirm what is safe to publish vs keep in a gitignored local note.
items.notes: Needs Nick input on what tunnel/Access detail is publishable. Not auto-safe (public repo + infra secrets).

items.body: MED observability + LOW reliability (bundled). scripts/start-all.ps1 runs node + cloudflared with -WindowStyle Hidden, no logging, and health-checks only `Get-NetTCPConnection -State Listen -LocalPort 4517` (start-all.ps1:13-17). So a silent crashloop is invisible and a wedged-but-listening process never relaunches. Add: (1) a timestamped line appended to a rotating log on each relaunch; (2) a cheap GET / liveness probe with short timeout that relaunches on non-200 as well as no-listener; (3) a tunnel last-seen/up field in status/data/Agents.json via write-dashboard-status.ps1 so a silent outage shows on yesandeverything.com/status. Files: X:\YesAndAgents\scripts\start-all.ps1, scripts/write-dashboard-status.ps1.
items.notes: Watchdog hardening; pairs naturally with the dirty-tree-dashboard-flag item (same writer).

_drain_log.note: _drain_log history lost to FUSE write-truncation during loop tick 560 queue status-flip; reconstructed empty. items array verified intact (count preserved). Rotate _drain_log to a sidecar on next attended drain.
